Perfume rating 4.18 out of 5 with 30,027 votes

Reviews (3.0K)

Tobacco Vanille by Tom Ford is a Oriental Spicy fragrance for women and men. Tobacco Vanille was launched in 2007. The nose behind this fragrance is Olivier Gillotin. Top notes are Tobacco Leaf and Spicy Notes; middle notes are Vanilla, Cacao, Tonka Bean and Tobacco Blossom; base notes are Dried Fruits and Woody Notes.

Read about this perfume in other languages: Deutsch, Español, Français, Italiano, Magyar, Nederlands, Polski, Português, Română, Srpski, Svenska, Türkçe, Čeština, Ελληνικά, Монгол, Русский, Українська, עברית, العربية, 日本語, 汉语, 한국어.

Perfumer

Olivier Gillotin

What People Say

Pros

Perfect for the winter season

High-quality, luxurious ingredient profile

Exceptional longevity and performance

Great for special occasions

Strong projection attracts frequent compliments

True unisex appeal

Warm, cozy, and comforting scent profile

Authentic, high-quality pipe tobacco scent

Cons

Very expensive price point

Polarizing, love-it-or-hate-it scent profile

High prevalence of online counterfeits

Too potent for daily wear

Heavy clove or cinnamon notes

Can be cloyingly sweet and heavy

Potential performance drop in newer batches

Linear development may feel boring to some

Dries down to a simple food-like vanilla

Note: These pros and cons are AI-generated from member reviews and may be inaccurate. Please read full reviews and consider your own needs before purchasing.

Jackie Beanstalk 18 hours ago

SkyDaddy 2 days ago

love
moderate
strong

Loji 2 days ago

Tobacco Vanille doesn’t give off a smoker’s or cigarette smell, despite what the name might suggest. Instead, it opens earthy, slightly sweet, and strong enough to last through the night. The tobacco leaf note is realistic and blends beautifully with the edible-style vanilla. As it settles, I pick up hints of wood, tonka bean, and cocoa. These stay in the background, supporting the main notes without taking over. The final dry down is quite dry but remains impressively persistent.

Not blind buy safe.

Everyone's nose / skin is different. Your experience may differ.

Perfumeart-NL 04/11/26 19:06

Suggestion: Can we, as the world biggest fragrance community, step up and visit ifra or Tom ford? Or maybe both:p? And ask them politely to bring back all the good stuff like; Tobacco oud, tobacco oud intense, oud wood intense, plum japonais etc….


And also make all the private blend stronger like the used to be! Because it hasn’t been that long ago (2018/2019) that I sprayed oud wood and it lasted about a week on my clothing. And of course I’m not even talking about the first batches (2007/2014), those were like spraying pure perfume oil:p.

Or the first time I sprayed Tuscan leather, never forget it had a sillage round 18 hours….. I really miss those moments…


Ps…. Of course I would like to see this for more brands and perfumes like Gaultier2, Dior homme intense, Dior homme parfum, bvlgari pour homme etc…..

Nice nasopharyngeal 04/10/26 20:35

like

Cologne Cathedral 04/09/26 06:11

Smells way too feminine initially for me.
quite strong. Sweetness.
not really getting much if any vanilla.

Annoyingly gourmand like a toffee at points.

Dry down is of course better.
Actually quite addictive.

dry down smells like such a delectable sort of spiced cookie.
It's that tobacco note but it's actually really quite nice.
certainly quite addictive. quite sweet.
It's difficult to say no when it smells like that...
Does have a slightly slight mature aspect to it.
Not quite there though.

Not a daily driver but unlike so many other fragrances it genuinely smells really nice so it's hard to say no!

dislike

basswuubs 04/07/26 18:24

smells like old fruits

love
long lasting
strong

sarin_sunshine 04/07/26 16:04

I've been absolutely dying to get my nose on this scent for basically the last year. Despite its high price tag, something about it has called to me since I started this hobby. Been wanting to get a decant for a while, but it just never worked out that way. Finally pulled the trigger because I found a sealed bottle for about $260 shipped, which is the lowest I've peeped since I started looking.

Aaaaand I've smelled it before. And that's putting it lightly. Smelling this was almost like unlocking a core memory that's been hidden away for years.

I'm 90% sure that I've smelled this on other people before in the wintertime, but there's also a very distinct possibility that I'm wrong, because this smells like any number of other things too. People have said it of this and other fragrances, but this is straight Christmas in a bottle. First thought that came to my mind when I smelled it was a Christmas tree, even if it doesn't smell like a tree would. I definitely get the tobacco and vanilla, but the dried fruits definitely take precedence over the vanilla for me. And those dried fruits smell kind of like those bunches of cinnamon pine cones that they start putting out around Halloween/Thanksgiving time. But there's also a very sleek tobacco smell, less like a cigar and more like that nice, moist tobacco you get when opening a fresh pack of cigarettes. I don't think there's a boozy note in here, but the notes wrap together in a way that almost makes it smell like a liqueur of dried fruits, cinnamon, and tobacco. Or maybe a compote would be more accurate.

This fragrance also ages beautifully. Got batch code A91, which my usual batch checking website says was created nearly 6 years ago. No sign of aging though. In fact, it might have even gotten better with age. No alcoholic harshness on initial spray, just pure opulence. This isn't the tobacco fragrance I was expecting or even hoping for, but it is a masterpiece in every sense of the word. A solid 9.5. I think Tobacolor is my personal favorite tobacco scent, but this is a worthy contender, and a great addition to my collection. I'll be wearing this on every Christmas going forward.

love
moderate
intimate

adriandaimhín 04/07/26 15:11

this is one of my favourites in my collection and i totally get why its such a popular classic. this is a lovely simple deep spicy sent thats warm and sweet without being too sickly or feminine. makes me feel like the sexiest dude ever.

toothdecade 04/07/26 06:44

like
moderate
moderate

ArdaYsl 04/06/26 10:11

When I try to think this scent as a person, I think about a mysterious man with a black coat or leather jacket, but usually the users of this scent are wasting it as a daily scent. I mean of course wear whatever you like but come on man tobacco vanille isn't a scent to use as a daily, it's more like something special. But about the fragrance, it's very good, I like it alot, such an original scent, would be better if there wasn't much users of it. About the scent, its really the name of the scent, tobacco and vanilla, don't worry the scent doesn't smell like an ashtray unlike red tobacco (God, I hate that scent). It is well blended and slightly sweet, it would be a bit better if it wasn't that expensive for a designer brand. I give an 8/10 for this.

jlog23 04/05/26 07:49

I wasn’t jiving with spices in this one. Pass for me.

eternal

Bullet Noise 04/03/26 10:55

smells exactly like the title. it’s a polarizing scent too. my dad didn’t like this cuz he said it just smells way too much like tobacco. the only thing that i didn’t expect was the cacao note and the sweetness of the whole fragrance. this has almost a chocolate smell blended in there that stands out more than you’d expect. idk if i’m the only person that picks up on it. i really like this but it doesn’t have me in a chokehold like some other stuff does.

i easily get 24 hours+ with this on skin. even after a shower i can still smell it on my skin. well worth the price i’d say for the quality you get, longevity, and projection. you dont need a lot of sprays so a 50ml will probably last you a while if it’s not a signature.

Beautiful, luxurious fragrance. Shame about the price.

love
eternal
enormous

itskamon 04/01/26 08:50

Definitely a mature scent, reserved for men in their late 20's and beyond as to some younger crowds it can smell like a backwoods. for a 28 year old, its a distinguished 'correct-for-my-age' fragrance that, as the reviews say, you should wear on special occasions. You really either love it or hate it, and I fall int he former category. opens with the tobacco scent and dries down into a sweet vanilla with earthy tones. I would imagine wearing this to a night time meeting of any sorts, suit and tie event with an open area (it could project too much in an enclosed space), bar or lounge (at night) or anything of sophistication.

love
long lasting
enormous

Jiriruca 03/31/26 14:55

This was my signature scent for almost 10 years (as a woman). To say that I adore it is putting it lightly. It was the first ever bottle of perfume I ever got gifted and the only one I wanted to own for years. I was so strongly associated with this scent in my late teens-early 20s that people said they remembered me when they sniffed this at stores. This is the scent I remember the first time I drank alcohol or when I had my first kiss. For me, it's full of memories and indissociable from them. It's long lasting, but at some point all my clothes and furniture had traces of it seeing as it was the only stuff I wore.

For me, it is a classic. I understand how it can be polarizing. It projects a lot at first and I get how the middle tonka and cocoa can feel super heavy for some mixed with the tobacco. I might also love it because I'm a smoker and it blended very well with the cigarette smell but, throughout the years, I was complimented on it tons and told it matched my personality. Perhaps that's why I loved it and felt like it made sense for me - I was also a quite polarizing person. There's something about it that contains very perfectly the final years of the 2000s going into the 2010s and whatever was in the air back then. I don't know how I'd feel about it if I was trying it out now for the first time - who's to say I wouldn't hate it, too.

I will always own this bottle and wear it sometimes, despite not having it be my signature anymore. It holds a special place in my heart and likely always will. It combines a loud teenage rebellion with a softness and complacency that I appreciate and identify with. It's not for the faint of heart and it's not a fragrance most people will like on themselves. It's not a blind buy and I'd recommend it for people who enjoy standing out to some extent. It makes a statement that you can love or hate, but it makes it extremely well.

dislike

Bunnillion 03/29/26 11:16

I wanted to love this one so bad, it seemed like a perfect scent for me and to rub salt in the wound it was a gift from my wife. Unfortunately, I just cannot get over the cough medicine quality of the tonka bean here, it made me averse to buying anything that has tonka bean for a long while. I kept some of it in a vile before giving the bottle to my nose blind father who thinks everything smells good enough for him, it matured in that vile for a year and was mixed with 2 other vanilla/patchouli perfumes. Now that mix was divine, makes me want to give this scent another chance, but that's not gonna happen anytime soon.

Update: The drydown is magnificently creamy. I take back everything I said, even though the opening is aggressive.

Aron09 03/28/26 08:52

Incredible fragrance that gives a nice scent of woody notes and vanilla. It gives a strong feel with the woody— and spicy notes, but still comes around soft with the vanilla; A quite fine balance.
Perfect for autumn/winter and works out well for both daily wear and special occasions.
The price might cause rejection, but if one finds themselves capable of paying such a price, then one might realize that it makes it worth.

r3ll129 03/17/26 08:33

I'm not a fragrance connoisseur by any means and this is my first review but I have to write it because I'm extremely disappointed in this one. It has all the notes which I thought I love; vanilla, tobacco, wood, tonka and even cacao, but I'm so glad I decided to a sample and not a blind buy a whole bottle because in my opinion it just doesn't work.The vanilla is very bitter, I guess you could say realistic, but I would prefer a softer, sweeter vanilla along with a more prominent cacao note to balance out the tobacco and spices. The dry down doesn't get much better for me as it stays very screechy and bitter. One thing I can say is that this thing PROJECTS (which was actually a bad thing in this case as I could smell it on myself all day and it borderline gave me a headache) and lasts a really long time on skin (12hrs plus).

zestysexysweet 03/11/26 08:52

Honestly it's an immediate vanilla, a very refined mid note tobacco, and the dry down reminds me of cinnamon and dried peaches. Like your very glamourous grandmother who started smelling like this in her twenties and never stopped.

Tramic 03/05/26 14:58

Oh my!!!! This is my daily. My very favorite.

Rich tobacco scent but it's not a fresh wet tobacco, kind of a washed cleaned tobacco scent, a dry tobacco leaf. Vanilla is prominent on the dry down. The spiciness is sharp and crisp. I get such a crisp clove scent. Anise is there but quiet. Spiciness just flows on and on. The tobacco and creamy vanilla just work so perfectly. Not overpowering, just there. So nicely there. Id like my house, my car, my friends houses and cars all to smell like this. I'll take a body lotion, air freshener, , after shave, a shampoo, soap and a deodorant all in TFTV scent, ty. I kinda like it. 😉

Scent_of_Pragmatism 02/25/26 06:29

like
long lasting
strong

Nathan86 02/22/26 18:54

A guilty pleasure. Just like that song that comes on the radio that you’ll belt along to in your car when you’re alone, but if anyone else is in the car with you you’ll scoff and switch the channel. I shouldn’t like this, but I do.

Really about the only thing tobacco-like to this perfume is the opening, which is a big hit of green tobacco leaf that is really pleasant. Already though the vanilla and the cocoa note are rushing into the room. At this point it does smell convincingly of a sweet and vanillic pipe tobacco, but once the greens disappear it is mostly a chocolate-patchouli and vanilla bomb that is sweet, crude, crass… and I like it. Sorry, not sorry. There’s a bit of booze, a bit of fruit, but nothing really truly convincing as such, like you might be smelling a cognac snifter a day or two after it actually had some cognac in it. The base goes more dark and aromatic woody thanks to guaiac wood, but make no mistake that the coumarin is still playing strong.

It’s not particularly well composed, the materials and their handling kind of suck, it’s lumpen and deadpan, it hits more gourmand targets than it does tobacco targets for sure, but… I don’t know… I just like it. The best way to experience this is minimally; don’t wear much of it, just a couple sprays - for the love of God, you beast mode, gym bro types, yes I’m talking to you: catching a brief whiff of this is far more pleasant than it clobbering you or me over the head, which it can do because it is very strong and tenacious. Additionally, as it is with nearly all of the Private Blend fragrances, this works remarkably well when worn with other Private Blends. A spray of this and a spray of Oud Wood creates a very stunning head-scratcher of a fragrance - but do it well and be smart, a spray of one there and a spray of the other over there, otherwise it could come across as quite messy.

So what do I really want to say about Tobacco Vanille? I don’t know. The person writing this review is a lover of jazz and classical music, of Stan Getz and Beethoven, but he also has a soft spot for Jimmy Buffett and Boston. Make of that what you will. Tobacco Vanille is in the latter camp, as far as I’m concerned, but you will still find me in the car belting along.

JuustoHirmu98 02/21/26 05:53

To me it honestly smells like the purest form of honey. I dont get tabacco or vanilla, my nose might just be inexperienced but all i get is this pure, very sweet honey smell. Otherwise decent.

emawoo 02/20/26 22:13

a grown ass man fall/winter fragrance. ts smell like a boss, i had a decant of this a while back and idk why i never got a full bottle of this. smoky vanilla done perfectly, 9.2/10

love

enormous

emre269 02/20/26 16:52

Tom Ford Tobacco Vanille is a very interesting fragrance where I can pick up cocoa, tobacco, and vanilla at different moments on my skin, which makes it quite fun to wear. It’s definitely a winter scent with strong projection that gets noticed from a distance. I don’t think it’s safe for blind buy, but those who love it will probably love it fully. When used in the right dose, it feels very charismatic. It truly shines in cold weather and evening wear.

Anon_Fragrances 02/16/26 10:58

Strong sexy vanilla, spices linger in the background with a small lovely tobacco, dried fruits are apparent, i dont know what to write about this fragrance its just so delicous

love
long lasting
strong

feeshy_freshy 02/15/26 22:12

Anyone who lives or grew up in the UK will immediately associate how TV smells with all things Christmas. Think mince pies, Christmas cake with brandy, church candles, mulled wine, a smoky fireplace, Christmas crackers, cigarettes after a work Christmas party. Tom Ford describes TV as "reminiscent of the very finest of private clubs" but I think this is a very languid description, mostly because it is not particularly heavy on the tobacco, and instead (at least for me) it's defined by the dried fruits and spices. To stifle any confusion: in the case of TV, that is absolutely a good thing. The T and V are there, but they simply round it out.

If you weren't wary already, beware of influencer opinions on this one. TV is often described as "dangerous" or "sexy" or "dark". I do not think any of these adjectives are remotely correct. TV is welcoming, warm, and cosy. This is sexy to a lot of people, so perhaps that one's valid, just not in the way it's made out to be.

I'm not really a fan of Tom Ford's private line. Except for this one. TF discontinued so many good perfumes from their private line but luckily held on to this. Go for the 30 or 50ml and try to find it below retail price, and you'll have plenty to come back to winter after winter.

Smell: 20/20
Versatility: 6/10
Performance: 10/10 (8-10h)
Value: 4/5
Bottle: 4/5
Verdict: 44/50

love

rednose 02/15/26 11:18

This and Lost Cherry are so fake smelling now and nowhere near as intoxicating as they were or as the orginal formula so much so that they make Istanbul by Atelier Rebul shine in comparison. It's like someone took the sexy out of her and now she's walking around with a claw foot.

For my money, the best fragrance ever created!!! When I first smelled this, all I could think about was spiced, smoked honey…that’s what I got from it and that’s all it needed to sell me! I love it and in my opinion, it’s universal for any season.

rfszk 02/14/26 13:40

I love when my husband wears this perfume, he smells so gorgeous and sexy in it. You can definitely pick up the tobacco and spicy notes, along with a sweet vanilla that feels more mature because of the tobacco. It’s not gourmand or cute at all , it’s warm, confident, and grown.

On me, though, it pulls much spicier, with only the faintest hint of sweetness, almost savory, somehow. It’s probably just my body chemistry, but I really don’t like how it smells on me.

dr.hamada 02/05/26 10:05

Finally, I got it (50 Ml) after years of hesitation. Well, to my nose (Contrary to widespread impressions), the opening burst with plum-like dried fruity note that strongly reinforces the gourmand impression, with the vanilla note in the background softening the accompanied spicy tobacco note, it makes it less harsh than tobacco note of Aramis Havana (One of my favorites). Despite its widespread popularity among other Tom Ford fragrances (PRIVATE BLIND), as beauty of smell to me it comes in second order after Noir de Noir, Arabian Woods, and the legendary Italian Cypress (Unfortunately the last two are out of production). It's not safe for blind buy. It's a winter fragrance (Unsuitable for summer at all). Regarding the performance I got 2024 patch with excellent longevity (6 hours on skin and +10 hours on cloths) and below average sillage (It becomes skin scent after 2.5 hours) which could be easily enhanced by applying 2 sprays each 2 hours.

fructoze_productions 02/05/26 01:06

There is a $15-20 dollar version of this sold at CVS from their "Perfect Scents Platinum Collection". I have never smelled the real thing so I can't tell if its close - but hopefully this is helpful to anyone who has and doesn't want to drop $200+ in case in does smell the same.

long lasting
strong

NicheFragster 02/04/26 17:11

On my skin, Tobacco Vanille is a rich, comforting cloud of warm tobacco leaf wrapped in mixed spices (very clove/cinnamon-leaning) and a creamy vanilla–cacao accord. It’s sweet but not cloying – more like spiced vanilla custard with pipe tobacco shavings than a boozy, smoky bar – and it stays smooth and mellow from start to finish. Projection is moderate-to-strong for the first couple of hours before it settles into a cozy, intimate aura, and I get around 6–8 hours on skin, with traces on clothes the next day. This is absolutely a cold-weather and evening scent (winter and chilly autumn/spring days are its home turf). If you enjoy warm, spicy vanilla gourmands with a refined tobacco twist and zero ashtray vibes, this is a gorgeous and addictive choice. 🍂🍦💯

hate

Andrexe25 02/04/26 11:43

Best vanilla fragrance ever made ! 10/10 but don't blind buy , it might not suit you , also many stores that have TV on display can be spoiled so I recommend smelling it at least 2-3 different times at different stores.

thenerabera 02/03/26 10:52

Dry fruity tobacco in a basket of cloves, with a little honey aspect and lots of vanilla.
10/10 fragrance. Not blind buy safe due to dryness, cloves and thickness of smell.
People complaining about longevity are crazy, maybe they have fakes?? Idk..
I have a 2024 50ml bottle, so batches should not matter so much, unless you have on that's at least a decade old.
It stays on my skin a minimum of 8 hours, projecting really nice.
Lasts on clothes until you wash them.

love

Sevestus 02/02/26 00:07

The cult classic, for good reason. "Christmas in a bottle", perhaps in the stereotypical sense. Maybe I can agree with that. It's just unfortunate that where I live, Christmas is accompanied by the sweltering summer.

Almost the blueprint for a whole genre of fragrances at this point, leading some to think there's nothing unique about it. I'd call this the "Aventus dilemma", but that'd be weird considering this is the senior. So, now that other things "smell like" this, or also achieve what it set out to do, that must mean that there's nothing special about Tobacco Vanille? Look, go for the alternatives by all means, but put some respect on the king's name.

Yes, it smells fantastic. Opening can be harsh to the unprepared, as the spicy qualities are highly concentrated there. Some will love that, some will hate it. Otherwise, you're getting an actually realised version of this formula done right. Warm, sweet and long-lasting. It's the perfect package for this scent profile.

love

fairy fragrances 02/01/26 02:00

I really wanted to love this perfume but it just smells like spraying vanilla perfume after a workout, there’s an odd bo smell to it

love

Arvin Ahmadi 01/31/26 06:00

Tom Ford Tobacco Vanille is autumn in a bottle, that is, as best as I can describe it. The scent and vibe this gives off just reminds me of falling orange leaves on a cold autumn afternoon while wearing a very sophisticated outfit. This is old money in the cooler and cloudy season. Rich vanilla, smooth tobacco, and added spices and woods make this a perfect signature in the autumn and winter time if you are quite classy and refined. Smells quite gourmand, but it is perfectly unisex, an absolute must-have for the person wearing Chelsea boots, a chunky knitted sweater, leather gloves, and even a brown bag wrapped around them. Tobacco Vanille doesn't announce your presence; it invites it, makes people want to get closer and closer to you to smell it. This could honestly be a huzz magnet if you got that right aesthetic on you. Trying to capture that old money, preppy aesthetic in orange autumn vibes? Tobacco Vanille is yours.

love
long lasting
moderate
+1

MaryMaloof 01/30/26 09:40

I’m going to do another review of Tobacco Vanille, a perfume I absolutely love. It was one of the first “expensive” fragrances I ever tried, and the one that really broke tradition for me. My first unisex perfume, and honestly, my first crush on Tom Ford.

The first time I smelled it was through a blind buy. I live somewhere without perfume shops, so everything I own has to be ordered online. That first spray opened with a strong wave of spices—but not synthetic or sharp. It reminded me of an artisanal chai tea: warm, smooth, and delicious. Nothing aggressive like cinnamon hitting your nose. This is a refined, almost edible blend.

Soon after, I noticed a very realistic tobacco leaf note paired with a beautiful, edible vanilla. As it dries down, hints of wood, tonka bean, and cocoa begin to appear, but they never take over. They stay subtle, supporting the composition rather than dominating it. The final dry down is quite dry, yet incredibly persistent.

I love wearing this fragrance when I’m dressed up—it honestly feels like a crime to wear it with simple clothes. This is a scent for empowered men or women. Think long leather coat, red lipstick, a blue-and-white striped shirt tucked into wide-leg jeans, finished with a leather belt. You have to dress to impress to truly do this perfume justice.

I love it. I adore it!

strong

EtherM 01/30/26 08:24

Tinny, two-note, mass-produced yucky stuff, when there are a million wonderful niche scents to explore ON THIS SITE that have oodles more originality, complexity and DELICIOUSNESS. Don’t settle.

Ameliahssbhvbh 01/29/26 05:20

Very spicy at the beginning, slowly with time becoming much more intimate and subtle. This is that kind of perfume you will never find again. It smells like a green tea on a rainy day. Very comforting fragrance, nostalgic almost. Tobacco, clove, tea, middle of November. Magical feeling locked in a bottle of perfume.

9nja 01/28/26 05:31

I really wanted this to work on my skin, but unfortunately, it comes across as too sweet on me. That said, it’s clearly a very well-constructed fragrance, with excellent projection and impressive longevity.

What surprised me most was just how sweet it turned out to be; I was expecting something different going in. On my skin, I don’t pick up much in the way of spices or woods, though that may simply be a skin-chemistry thing.

If you enjoy very sweet tobacco fragrances, this is probably one of the best options out there in my opinion — it just isn’t for me.

Update: It GOT BETTER THIS IS PHENOMENAL!!

Update: It's FIRE i love this. vanilla is super syrupy and the tobacco is smooth and overall this fragrance smells like a masala chai tea bag drizzled with vanilla extract and dipped in the boiled water mug that has cinnamon and a touch of honey in there, you stir it all up and the steam makes THIS scent. very syrupy sweet, tobacco spicy, vanillic, and warm.

cankuta 01/18/26 22:26

Heavy for summer but amazing in cold weather.

moderate

zamiulmehrab 01/18/26 08:43

Nothing to say much about this perfume, smells like vanilla essence with a slight woodiness and sprinkles of dried tobacco leaves.Easily lasts 10+hour on my cloth(i never spray perfumes on skin). The downside for me is that the perfume has minimal amount of shift of phase, to me the starting and the end smell felt the same; still tobacco vanille will be one of the timeless fragrance for the fall/winter for me.

MutMaat 01/18/26 05:17

"Tobacco Vanille" opens with a fruity tobacco accord, delicately sweet and spicy. I can feel dried apricots, raisins and cranberries, with the slightly sour taste, sugary and just a touch honeyed.
With advance of tonka and cacao the scent becomes richer and velvet-like, comforting and weaving an image of an old plush library armchair in one of the aristocratic grand houses of Old Engalnd. It has the whiffs of tobacco smoked the night before still permeating the air, with a billiard table across the rooms and hundreds of leather-bound tomes lining antique mahogany or oak shelves.
Vanilla and tobacco blossom are not that noticeable in the composition during the Fall-Winter period, there are hints of them in the roundness of spices and woods, but that is all. When worn in Spring - Summer, - vanilla is more "confident" in her stance, especially during the drydown. But she is never cloying or overbearing, nor gourmand-like.
Fragrance' projection is a sensible one, not shouting or punching, but being present and noticeable all the same. It becomes alike of a curled up sleeping cat when transforming into the skin scent, and just like the cat - lingers on for hours and hours.
"Tobacco Vanille" is all about aristocratic luxury, the almost inevitable continuation of the bloodlines and the innate understanding and knowledge of elegance, tasteful opulence and centuries-long class of its own existence.

hate
long lasting
strong

Akimm 01/18/26 03:43

I don't like this fragrance at all,the tobacco here gives me a headache. I can't smell vanilla here because of the tobacco.

DakotaKP22 01/17/26 22:50

So Hudson Williams smells like extreme Christmas spice Egg Nog, got it.

like
long lasting
moderate

UniScent Springbok 01/17/26 14:31

I have read so much about this fragrance which is so popular.... and finally got to try it out recently at Duty-Free in an airport. The label does not lie. It is tobacco, and it is vanilla. Extremely realistic, quality raw tobacco leaves, and sweet vanilla. And to me, that was it. Way too strong tobacco, and boring with the overpowering 2 notes. No complexity, no balance. It is not that I don't like tobacco... but romanticize it a bit in a fragrance. I must admit, that I only tested the opening in the shop... but cannot imagine that the drydown would be so completely different.

PoatanFrags 01/16/26 23:21

Goat cologne, just don’t have it. 10/10 must have.

Swank 01/16/26 08:15

Smells like a Stewart's cream soda / root beer with good sweetness that isn't annoying. Very pleasant smell probably most fitting for winter.

ElifAkyol 01/16/26 07:46

I layer this with VS Bare Sueded Vanilla and I like it so much. BSV first TV over it after a couple of minutes

Xowah 01/15/26 17:56

When I first got a perfume I really wanted the smell of a cigar shop, so I looked into something with patchouli. This used to be my favorite, I first got a tester of it and decided I loved it until I got the bottle and quickly got sick of it. The patchouli is honestly too much for me. Very jarring and sickly. I will say the silage on this is insane, it has a strong scent. Unfortunately I can't get over the patchouli, the fragrance is too warm, jarring, and nauseating for me. And the perfume is so expensive. I would recommend a tester of this, you absolutely can't blind buy this. Super intense for the better or for worse!

Die.Dream.Life 01/10/26 13:38

Tom Ford Tobacco Vanille is a warm, intimate whisper of smoke and sweetness.
Rich tobacco leaf drifts softly into creamy vanilla, wrapped in spice and amber, creating a scent that feels both refined and deeply human.
It lingers like a quiet memory—dark, elegant, and comfortingly bold.

Devotion Magic 01/09/26 18:08

The price is crazy but I do genuinely think this is one of the nicest tobacco scents out there! The vanilla is also gorgeous, almost like scraping vanilla beans. Will purchase a travel size one day!

MojoCoco85 01/08/26 03:46

My husband wears this, its 10/10, honestly intoxicating in the best way possible. Lasts and projects but not offensively. If you want the females species stuck to you like glue, men wear this.

WildCalifornia 01/08/26 03:09

The full dry down isn't bad but everything before that comes off sour to me . Almost like spoiled tobacco and vanilla. On skin and a test strip.

FeaRdZINe 01/07/26 18:10

Received a sample of this from Macy's and I think @HoodyWooderson described it perfectly as a brutal elegance. I'd say the opening is like slamming your head into a wall of tobacco leaves but the dry down is where it shines. Once the vanilla starts to seep through it blends amazingly with the smell of dark spicy tobacco. When I smell this I think of Vegas. Do not blind buy.

Mike1341 01/06/26 18:27

As alot of said already, it’s a polarizing scent that stands out as soon as you spray it on. It will remind you of Christmas or a scented candle right away. After wearing it awhile , I understand why it’s loved and hated. You will definitely turn heads when wearing it. It’s something about the smell that is just so special, so beautiful, but it’s not a scent that you can spray on and wear everyday. It’s better for special occasions.

DarthFragrance 01/04/26 05:32

I love the smell, but it's one of those fragrances I'd mostly wear alone to appreciate by myself. My gut feeling is that people would not appreciate this in public. It's a dated and challenging scent. It's heavy and cloying. Basically vanilla, tobacco, cloves and dried fruits. Unique and easily recognizable. I just don't feel like others would appreciate it and you'd be better off just being fresh and clean with no fragrance than wearing this in public. I've bought it twice and gotten rid of it both times. First time, I exchanged it for Carlisle which was a good decision. I can't remember what happened with the other. I do love it, though, and I would like to have it. At the same time, I don't want it. A love hate relationship.

Rosegold 01/03/26 10:10

I have a 30ml decant from a split almost 15 years ago. I only wear it once or twice per year, usually around the holidays. It’s a bore to wear on its own but makes a good base for layering, which I believe was the whole purpose of the line anyways. I sprayed one spritz at the back of my neck. 24 hours later it’s still there.

JohnnyFreshFrag 12/30/25 12:27

@Karlsscents

This dude reviewed the fragrance negatively twice in the last year. I understand fragrances are subjective, but this dude has Dior Sauvage as the greatest designer cologne ever made, so take that as you will 😂

In regards to TV, it’s a beautiful and well blended fragrance that is not meant for the younger crowd, but more so someone 30+ in my opinion. It’s elegant, mature, sexy, and very strong. I can see why it can be a polarizing scent, but I think this is the type of fragrance that is for someone with a more acquired and developed taste. The vanilla is more forth coming on my skin over the tobacco, which is a good thing. Perfect for winter date nights or going out. Not blind guy worthy at all. Longevity is decent as well. Price point is just too high for this type of fragrance in my opinion. For that, I give it a 8.5/10

Scratparry 12/28/25 10:07

Simply amazing scent. First time I smelt this when getting into fragrances my first thought was was who the hell would enjoy wearing that.
Now it’s one of my favourites.
Putting this on for an evening out really makes you feel elevated and helps you stand out.
Performance on me is fantastic, I’m still getting whiffs 12 hrs after initial spraying (around 3-4 sprays)

Fill retail is £222 as of time of writing for 50ml is bit too much IMO. However being one of TF’s most popular scents I’ve always seen it on discount so if you can grab it this way it’s a new brainer. Grab it!

ElysiumYouth 12/26/25 21:16

Don't own it, but my brother has a sample. I sniffed it off his hand a while back, and with his bodychem at least, it really smells like coca cola; it's mainly coke, and then there's some cigarette smoke. I mean that as a major compliment, I love both those scents so so much!, and they’re blended well together. I especially like it since the coke here is just plain coke, no lime or cherry like so many other fragrances tend to add.

MonaFialka 12/25/25 13:17

I bought it for my husband, but it is so good I use it on my own. I can detect vanille, tonka, cacao, tobacco and rhum, but predominantly to me it is a tonka centered perfume. It is a warm, full, well blended composition. It states luxury, confidence and also it holds a huge portion of sexappeal. I would say it is mouthwatering and if you are going on date, this is a secret superpower for seducing anyone (man or woman, to me it is truly unisex). Only downside - I doesn't last all day as other people describe. Anyway, angelic winter smell!

love
long lasting
enormous

TheDogfatherHTX 12/24/25 00:17

Today’s review marks a return to the house of Tom Ford, this time with one of its most recognizable and frequently discussed creations, Tobacco Vanille. Often regarded as a cornerstone of the brand’s Private Blend lineup, this fragrance has become closely associated with deep sweetness, warm spice, and a bold, enveloping presence. Built around the interplay of rich tobacco leaf and creamy vanilla, it suggests a profile that is equal parts indulgent and commanding, evoking imagery of cigars, spiced confections, and dimly lit lounges rather than fresh or restrained elegance. My first experience with this DNA did not come directly from Tom Ford, but through a clone, Al Haramain Amber Oud Tobacco Edition, which offered an early introduction to the general character long before encountering the original itself. Returning to Tobacco Vanille now carries a different sense of curiosity, shaped by familiarity with the style but not the source. Let’s light it up and see how this iconic blend of tobacco and sweetness reveals itself once worn.

Opening:
The opening is sweet, spicy, and warm. The most prominent notes are tobacco, vanilla, spices, and cacao, with tobacco and vanilla clearly taking center stage. True to the name, the tobacco announces itself immediately, presenting an intensely natural, sweet, and warmly textured tobacco accord. Having recently begun exploring cigars, this opening instantly calls to mind the aroma of a high-quality luxury cigar, rich, smooth, and unmistakably authentic. Vanilla arrives alongside it in equal measure, folding a decadent, creamy sweetness into the tobacco and softening its edges without diluting its character. A blend of warm spices follows, non-specific in isolation but unmistakably evocative of fall baking spices such as cinnamon, nutmeg, clove, and allspice. Cacao rounds out the opening quietly in the background, adding a subtle chocolaty depth that enriches the composition rather than drawing attention to itself.

Dry Down:
The dry down remains sweet, warm, and spicy, with tobacco and vanilla continuing to dominate the composition. Tobacco Vanille proves to be a fairly linear fragrance, as the overall structure and tone remain consistent from opening to dry down. The most noticeable shift comes from the replacement of cacao with dried fruits, introducing a soft, understated fruitiness that suggests notes like dates or raisins. This nuance adds a gentle dimension without changing the core identity of the scent. Beyond that, the fragrance stays true to its name, delivering a cozy, indulgent blend of warm tobacco and rich, creamy vanilla that remains smooth and enveloping throughout the wear.

Performance:
Projection during the first hour is extremely strong, easily filling a room and establishing a dense, commanding presence. Sillage matches that intensity, leaving a heavy and unmistakable scent trail. After three to four hours, projection remains largely unchanged, continuing to perform with confidence. Around the eight to nine hour mark, projection settles to roughly three to four feet, with the scent trail becoming lighter but still clearly noticeable. The fragrance eventually transitions into a skin scent around the eleven to twelve-hour mark. Overall, performance is excellent and fully in line with the fragrance’s bold and opulent character.

When to Wear:
Tobacco Vanille is unequivocally a fall and winter fragrance, best reserved for evening wear. Its richness and density make it particularly well-suited for special or formal occasions, where its luxurious profile can be fully appreciated. This is not a casual scent, nor does it feel appropriate for office wear. It thrives in settings where warmth, indulgence, and presence are welcomed rather than restrained.

Blind Buy:
This is not a safe blind buy. Tobacco Vanille is loud, assertive, and unapologetically bold, with powerful tobacco and rich vanilla taking center stage throughout its lifespan. Combined with its premium price point, it demands a level of confidence and commitment from the wearer. Sampling is strongly recommended before considering a full bottle.

Final Verdict:
Tobacco Vanille earns its reputation as one of the most celebrated tobacco fragrances on the market. As someone who genuinely enjoys tobacco-forward scents, experiencing it on skin makes it immediately clear why it receives the level of praise it does. The tobacco accord is rich, natural, and beautifully executed, elevated further by decadent vanilla and warming spices that give the fragrance depth and indulgence without feeling cluttered. In a market saturated with mass-produced and often uninspired tobacco fragrances, Tobacco Vanille stands apart as something distinctive and memorable. It leaves a lasting impression, both in performance and character, and fully lives up to its hype. This is a fragrance that will confidently earn a place in my personal collection.

Scent: 10/10
Longevity: 8/10
Sillage: 10/10
Versatility: Low
Mass Appeal: Average
Gender: Masculine
Value: Overpriced

tsolum 12/22/25 12:11

Perfect scent profile

Longevity without getting noseblind. I can feel it all day even after 10 hours.

Never duped, i've tried most but not all. They suck compared to.

One of the few perfect 10/10 for me.

moderate
moderate

Ayman61 12/22/25 08:44

Perfect smell but not long-lasting as I expected

Le Quiry 12/21/25 10:44

3 main notes you can smell in this fragrance:
1. Vanilla, which is very gourmand, creamy and smooth.
2. Tobacco which smells very herbal and at the same time cherry-like.
3. Cloves, which links the smell of vanilla and tobacco together and makes it spicy.
This is a very natural smelling fragrance. Extremely well blended and long lasting.
It deserves its high rating.

love

PapaGainz 12/21/25 08:40

This is old school tobacco meeting with modern mass appealing vanilla.

This is definitely a fragrance for the masculine nose, it is a vanilla cigar.
A example of a male fragrance to appeal to the women's nose would be Pdms althier. Not saying this is something to attract dudes or women don't like it, but often or not I wear this one purely cause I like it. I think it smells great, and gives me such a boss vibes. It is not something I wear for compliments for sa, even though you'll get them. It is just not as mass appealing as your modern sweet vanilla fragrances.

This fragrance has 3 main players that infuse together more, and more as the fragrance drys down...

1) Tobacco: the tobacco in here is raw, musicaline, almost dirty like. If you smoke cigars or used raw tobacco leafs to roll, it is exactly that kind of tobacco.

2) The vanilla: it is a nice, not overly sweet vanilla. It is there to being softness to the tobacco.

3) Spices: fall time spices to be more specific. It smells like stores around Christmas time. It got some cinnamon and woody nuances, along with raisins, and dried fruits.

At the beginning you can smell the 3 players as they are layerd on eachother with the tobacco being the strongest, as the fragrance drys down they all below and infuse together into a masterful blending. It is a very nice fragrance.

A lot of people might be repulsed by how they tobacco is here, it is not just a supporting elements or overly sweet. At the same time some people might be repulsed by the vanilla Christmas latte under the tobacco... For them they should try TFs Tobacco Oud.

Overall, this is a very nice fragrance, that also last and performe well.

love
long lasting
strong

MichealVoltaire 12/19/25 16:06

This fragrance is a favourite of mine and has been my signature for a while. Trust me when I say that tumeric latte by lush is a wonderful dupe for it!!!

It is slightly warmer than tobacco vanille, but other than that absolutely identical to me. If you love TF but don't love the price, try lush (do note that tumeric latte is seasonal limited edition only available around Christmas time)

luxlex 12/07/25 21:39

I like it as a winter scent. Outstanding staying power and with sound projection. You can get away with it being more subtle by not applying much (novel idea, I know!). It's very unisex, maybe leaning a little masculine. I don't let that steer me away from wearing it. Its slight masculine character won't override a properly contrasting outfit, or the perfumes' salient, flavorful, gourmand vanilla as its complementary feminine. A unisex character that a woman can wear to build an atmosphere around her at select events.

That said, its lean towards masculine-unisex may dissuade women from trying it. I personally wouldn't recommend it outside of select winter events at night. It's not for a rowdy club or a date night. Think of a cold night, where its spice notes evoke a sense of heat that amplifies the idea of feminine warmth. Probably not worth its price tag due to this limitation. If you can get a small sample, it is something to have as a special tool for the right night; you will know when.

99T9T999 12/07/25 17:07

Here is a basic description of how it smells.

It's like you are a very sexy block of honeycomb.

I think this is a good signature scent for men trying to make a statement or something like that.

9/10

like

marianneg23 12/07/25 10:12

I love and hate Tobacco Vanille.

Upon first spraying, it is rather divine. The spice, tobacco, sweetness, and woodiness come together for something bold and heavy, but clean at the same time. After a few hours, the sweetness overdevelops, the spice disappears, and the tobacco is but a whisper, and it cloys. It's like the guilty, sticky aftertaste of gorging yourself on chocolate cake.

Longevity and sillage are remarkable, and I wear this fragrance for going out in the fall and winter. I do have to time my application right - I want to be home and in the shower before it gets too sweet!

love
long lasting
strong

Strange718 12/06/25 22:49

One of my favorites😍

Flex Luther 12/05/25 09:29

I’ve always seen this fragrance in stores and steered away because of designer. I had my noise up like a lil niche snob. Yesterday I received Tobacco Vanille, Roja pour Homme and vanilla planifolia 21.

I sprayed vanilla planifolia 21 on one lady and I sprayed the other 2 on the other. I was putting the parfumes back in their boxes and I just heard behind me from the other room. Mmm mmm mmm 🤣 strange.. then a intoxicating whiff of a scent brushed by me, I ignored it and when in about 3 different room and kept getting whiffs of this vanilla smell, I was even upstairs and could smell it in a different bathroom I was in then I said. Bear in mind I didn’t spray it on me and it didn’t get on my finger. “ I bet that’s the vanilla planifolia 21so I rushed back down a said lemme sniff ya wrist” smelt vanilla planifolia 21 and it wasn’t that. The Roja I didn’t bother as I’m pissed it’s a freshy and not my thing good tho.

Then I smelt the TF TV, and BLUDKLART!!

The smell was so gorgeous and I couldn’t believe it was so strong and I realise it’s the resin in it and clove that are catching my attention with the Vanilla. Nearly on par with Vanagloria 👌🏾if your a vanilla head try that. But wait about 20mins cos the opening is arse but the dry down is cream powdery buttery Co2 extracted Vanilla.

Thank me later people.

Easy

love

Memesmellsvanilla 12/03/25 16:14

When i smelled it before like a year ago, i didn't like it, again that was because i was looking for a specific linear scents, Now when i was looking for different scents i like it so much.
Breakdown of the scent its the perfect spicy gourmand (along with khamra)
You can smell the tobacco which is coming off smoothly, and a hint of vanilla, it's spicy, alittle flowery and can i dare say sweetness? But it's not linear perfume for people who are looking for pure vanilla and ediable scent. It's like going to a jazz cafe listening to music and eating spicy food, feeling warm and what the hell lightning a cigarette after the meal.

It's so good and definitely a must add to the winter collection, it's the perfect balance between muscline and feminine energy, i feel it layers well with khamra since they are spicy light gourmand, along with choco musk and bare vanilla for added sweet warm layer to it.

I got it as a gift and i love it.

love

Alexislikesfragrances 12/02/25 09:37

Now this is the true FUCKING FABULOUS. Longevity for me is insane, at least 9 hours of projection and fades into a skin scent. It’s very nice and simple, tobacco leaves with their earthy dark scent which adds richness to the vanilla just like caramel with salted butter.

AlexPurple 12/02/25 06:27

🌹 The overabundance of vanilla in the men's perfumery market has recently become threatening. It seems to me that the "stronger with you" line is enough, it is "masculine" in inensly the best vanilla. either Givenchy is a gentleman eau de parfum - it's pure black vanilla. everything else is a sickly feminine cloying sound. It's unnecessary. I understand that in this way perfumers are trying to "calculate" and create volume and durability for at least some fragrance, from those ingredients that have not yet been banned by IFRA. But this is an overabundance, while the fresh segment is dying. Without the right doses of salicillates, the citrus flavor will last for half an hour and no one will buy it.

love
long lasting
strong

Yahia77 12/02/25 04:33

This one is not a blind buy at all, same goes for any dupe of it. Me personally, i loved it when i first smelled it, then i found it too cloying a while later, then again i find myself loving it now, that just shows how polarizing this one is, its very rich and strong, heavy on the tobacco and dried fruits, spices, and that rich vanilla sweetness in the drydown, and you either hate it or you love it, even me, i sometimes hate it and sometimes love it. DO NOT BLIND BUY.

trmjkd989 12/01/25 14:58

Quick first impressions.

I ordered a few samples online, and this one is easily the best of the bunch.

Basically smells like a spicy vanilla candle, in the best way. I can't stop smelling it. Was worried that it would smell too harsh or "old man" but the sweetness in the vanilla stays strong.

Update: Letting it settle on my skin, the spices really start to pop out. Reminds me of Christmas or a cold windy Autumn day. Longevity is pretty solid too 6 hours still going strong.

Most Popular Positive Reviews

love

Gabler 03/08/18 09:15

It´s a little bit difficult to write down this review because of those feelings inside. I don´t want to be much personal, just sincere - because this is the scent of my love & our home.

For me, there is something very familiar about this perfume. It reminds me of my life, my memories, my man. All the cigarettes he smoked while he was listening to my stories. Fluffy vanilla latte on Sunday mornings, the taste of Old English dark fruit cake that I have been baking every Christmas. Hennessy cognac for goodnight /while we were listening to our favorite song - Change Is Gonna Come/.

Sweet home, sweet times.

Tobacco, cigarette smoke, cigar ash, delicately sweet vanilla, dried fruits, cloves, cinnamon - that´s what I love, what I want, what I need. That is Tobacco Vanille.

like

Raidthesaladbar 08/10/18 04:00

By now we have all come to the consensus that Tobacco Vanille is brilliant/mediocre/horrible (delete where appropriate). This being said, I'd like to briefly talk about a tangential issue concerning this fragrance, which is the following:

tobacco (eng.) vanille (fr.)

What am I to make of this? How do I pronounce it? Switching accents halfway through is inadvisable, since even my friends from Nouvelle-Orléans choke on their Diet Coke whenever I correctly pronounce "Guerlain" in the middle of an English sentence. No, you have to commit to one only.

My first option is, then, to leave "vanille" as it's supposed to be (French) and say the English word "tobacco" with an exaggerated Jean-Reno-style accent. However, I find it cumbersome to lug around the silk béret and fake moustache which are necessary to channel the appropriate level of pretentiousness.

Alternatively, you can follow TF's marketing and pronounce the whole thing in English - like a clueless American tourist clutching his phrase book in a Parisian café ("Oooh ay le Starbucks?"), blissfully unaware of the savage beating the waiter is fantasising about behind his smile.

Perhaps this is all some grand œuvre de trollage - Mr. Ford snickering into his computer screen as he watches reviewers humiliate themselves trying to pronounce his fragrance names. If that's the intent though, I suggest he take a page out of Hermès' book and go all out. In this age of Youtube, Un Jardin en Méditerranée still ranks as the funniest fragrance I've ever come across.

love
eternal
enormous

Saeka 01/29/19 01:54

best.natural.store2018 (151)
95.5% positive Feedback
Member since 2016
Location:Bulgaria

It says located in Bulgaria but actually US. I should have known.

I will re-buy TV from TOM FORD BOUTIQUE from now on. I’m sorry for posting here but I thought nesessary.

love
long lasting
moderate

angela2394 03/22/21 07:08

This perfume just screams “ I GOT MONEYYYYYYYY, AND I’M LETTING YOU KNOW”.
Heads will be turned wearing this perfume. A man wearing this perfume going out with his guy friends, will get the most attention. Everyone will look at him when the ober askes who to give the bill to.
A Woman wearing this perfume just screams “ I’m independent, and can take good care of myself “.
Wearing this scent to a normal 9-5 job will have your coworkers think you don’t need this job, and that you got stacks of money. LOL
The notes I pickup are: Spices, Vanilla, Cacao , Tobacco, and a little hint of Fruit wich makes the perfect blend.
I would personally wear this fragrance in the winter time or when I know that I will be in an airconditioned room. It last 8+ hours on my skin.

Although this is the perfect fragrance, it is NOT for the weak !!!

5/5 Star

love
eternal
enormous

DULAC de La Clauze 09/18/15 13:46

"Is it a gift to someone special—?"
"Yes, very. It is a gift to myself."
She takes a brief moment to smile as I maintain eye contact.
"The best kind! would you like me to wrap it for you?"
"No. Give it! it is to be enjoyed right away." I said to the amusement of the Sephora-Plage representative of Saint Tropez, all the while sampling this eau de parfum on my wrist, then on my chest, opening the collar of my dress shirt as she was seemingly leaning forward.

Tobacco Vanille unexpectedly took me back to a very private place, where laughter was cheap and colours saturated. I used to be on the Right at the time and perhaps a bit emotionally disconnected, and "She" was on the other far-end of the political spectrum. Beautiful, strong, opinionated and witty. Naturally we clashed—Genevieve called me an Imperialist and I called her a child—she stormed out of the debate and I followed ... but before we could understand what had taken hold over us, here we were, lustfully rolling in the grass of our University. She used to perfume herself with nothing but vanilla extract while I reeked of those Davidoff cigarillos I favour to this day...

But the real Tobacco Vanille is far more eloquent. Yet it is an idea refined to its simplest and purest form like the most accomplished architectural projects. This eau de parfum is a opulent Niche caprice. It is a manly unsmoked pipe tobacco—It's tin is flavoured with cloves, anise and wood sap that sensually tango with a moist and candid Bourbon vanilla, a creamy tonka bean and what must be Criollo (a very high quality cocoa that represents less than 3% of the world's production) imported straight from madagascar—There is also a very playful note which echoes the smell of a little Grand Marnier spilled on an old leather sofa and expands further the underlying theme of the old Gentleman's Club so cherished by Tom Ford. Tobacco Vanille is a simple way of saying very elaborate things.

...Genevieve and I would soon abandon our ambitions and made plans to elope to the complete dismay of our respective comrades, but tragedy would decide otherwise (To those of you who have known this kind of quiet rage and survived it, I salute you.).

"Magnifique!" Back in Saint Tropez, the Sephora representative had just woken me from my memoria loci, studying me with those dark almond shaped eyes and nervously playing with the tip of her tight, tight ponytail (...). To quote Oscar Wilde, "Everything in the world is about sex, except sex. Sex is about power." and Tobacco Vanille certainly adds to mine.

Now I must add it's newly released beard oil to my routine.

Most Popular Negative Reviews

dislike

maureen.mccabe 01/15/18 16:27

After wearing the delicious Tobacco Vanille sample for a couple weeks, I decided I was tired of smelling like a burnt vanilla cupcake. I got bored. Same story with Cafe Rose.

Tom Ford fragrances are long-lasting and initially enjoyable, but they lack complexity. Love the design of the bottle, but that isn't nearly as important as what's inside.

It's like dating that hunky handsome guy you've always dreamed of, and then realizing he has no deeper qualities and is, in fact, a very shallow and boring person to be around. You know you could never fall in love with a guy like that.

dislike

Scentrist 09/06/11 18:24

So what can I say that hasn't already been said about Tobacco Vanille? Probably a lot.

If you want to duplicate TV, here's what you do: Go to your local grocery. Get a bottle of decent vanilla extract (ok, even the really chemical smelling fake stuff might do in a pinch), add a touch of cinnamon, chai spice, ground ginger and some ground cocoa. Combine, decant, and spray. You'll smell like cookies, but you won't smell much different than the linear lines of this fragrance. And you'll save roughly $190.

Seriously, the over-emphasis on the wrong note is often what plagues Tom Ford's other scents as well (as much as I appreciate Amber Absolute, the emphasis on incense tends to overwhelm the amber). Here, the tobacco is a missing element or mere footnote in what should have been a well-orchestrated symphony. For so much promise, so little is experienced. This is a syrupy sweet confection that a walk around the mall will make kids wonder where the waffles are being served. This is far too linear, very unimaginative, synthetic-smelling and lacking in the focal ingredient. Too many others have done tobacco in a much more appreciable way, Serge Lutens Fumerie Turque comes to mind, Creed Tabarome not far behind. I can appreciate many of the Private Blend fragrances, but this completely misses the mark.

dislike

misssarah 11/09/13 10:18

To me, this smells exactly like the waft of synthetic vanilla you get when walking into a craft store like Michael's, or like Christmas room spray. I understand Tom Ford is supposed to use high quality ingredients, but I honestly wouldn't be able to tell the difference between this and a cheap vanilla candle in a blind sniff test.

dislike
eternal
moderate

Malenkig 06/17/16 23:41

I realize this review will be unpopular, but here goes:

When smelling the cap alone, I got a promising, rich tobacco scent, laced with a small but dry vanilla. Spraying however left me with an overly sweet and slightly nauseating scent that began as a vanilla scented IKEA candle and over the course of an hour turned more and more akin to slightly stale Dr. Pepper. There were other scents of course (sugar that with age has fused into one big lump, fudge, soda that's turned flat) but Dr. Pepper dominated my experience with this perfume.

It wasn't just me. The day (there wont be another) that I wore this scent, was my sisters birthday, and so I got several opinions from her friends at her birthday party. These were fairly evenly distributed between the subtle disapproval ("Ohh... I'm more of a floral person, I guess...") and the downright comical recoil and retching I got from her boyfriend in particular.

Normally I tend to think a scent just doesn't react well with my skin, when I stumble upon something this bad. However, reading the reviews here I'm leaning more towards that a reformulation took place, and that it removed everything (some tobacco notes for example) that wasn't super sweet in this fragrance. Whats left is poorly balanced and sadly nauseating.

dislike

Birnam_Wood 10/26/20 18:09

-

话梅 “huamei”, an Asian snack made of pickled ume/preserved plum: this is exactly what Tobacco Vanille smells like. Ask any east or southeast Asian you know, they'll agree.

I googled a little more, then found what's responsible for that distinctive scent of huamei(=Tobacco Vanille):

Licorice.

To replicate this scent with 100% precision, first you need some extremely tart fruits, too sour to be eat alone, let them dried up with salt, treat them with a shit load of licorice, sprinkle them with cinnamon and clove powder, and round off the rough edges with vanillin: now you got the perfect Tobacco Vanille opening.

The latter half of the journey, if you ask me, mimics another Asian drink: rose oolong tea.

Oolong tea is a type of half fermented "black" black tea, smells incredibly dry and bitter with a whiff of "rusty" fermented hint

Getting close to your skin, the bitter and dried core of Tobacco Vanille smells exactly like dried oolong tea leaves; smells from afar, it has a hidden rosy aura.

The dry down has lost its shape, just a vague mix of all aforementioned notes, maybe adding some oily-coconut-y coumarin, along with a few drop of honey adding a little depth and animalic hint.
